title: July 15
description: A seasonal and civic-friction noticing artifact about summer, winter, Arizona heat, traffic as loading, and a sharp aside on identity talk.

motifMap.meaning: The post notices seasons as both endless while present and vanished once past.
A place with less seasonal contrast still carries the same felt cycle of arrival, endurance, and disappearance.
Driving through the city becomes a stalled interface, a bodily patience problem.
The final line records an irritated boundary around identity-performance language.

excerpt: Living in a place with both summer and winter. Each felt as if it would never come, never end, and was gone in a flash.

editHistory.notes: Manual live RSS extraction performed with user permission. Classified from the actual post text as Desert Faith with Dust on the Sandal and Methodic Self-Watching because the post moves through weather, Arizona heat, traffic friction, and an irritated self-observed boundary in ordinary life.
